L1.area: Stream cipher core computation and keystream generation logic

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the stream cipher's block counter. The 32-bit counter can overflow when generating keystreams larger than 2^32 blocks or when seeking past this limit. When overflow occurs, the keystream wraps around and produces duplicate output, which is equivalent to nonce reuse and exposes the XOR of two plaintexts.

L3.area: ChaCha20 cipher core block generation and counter handling
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the ChaCha20 stream cipher's block counter. The 32-bit counter can wrap around when generating more than 2^32 blocks of keystream or when seeking past this limit, causing the cipher to produce duplicate keystream blocks. This is equivalent to nonce reuse and allows an attacker to recover the XOR of plaintexts encrypted under the same key and nonce.

ground_truth.reason: The ChaCha20 block counter is a 32-bit value that silently overflows when generating more than 2^32 blocks of keystream, causing keystream reuse equivalent to nonce reuse and exposing plaintext XOR.
